message:
fix(backend,orchestrator,storage,sync): extract routing_mode helper, harden shutdown and storage resilience, upgrade sync cursor to composite key

Backend routing (mod.rs):
- Extract routing_mode() function that takes adaptive_mode_active and
  rollout_forced_static flags and returns "adaptive" or "static", replacing
  inline conditional logic in evaluate_backend_selection
- rollout_forced_static now correctly overrides adaptive mode
- New test covers all four input combinations

Shutdown resilience (orchestrator.rs):
- PipelineCx::checkpoint() and CancellationToken::checkpoint() now check
  ShutdownController::is_shutting_down() first, enabling faster Ctrl+C
  response without waiting for the next cancellation token poll
- FinalizerRegistry bounded cleanup now spawns finalizer closures on
  dedicated threads with recv_timeout enforcement, preventing a blocking
  finalizer from stalling the entire shutdown sequence
- Simplify budget_timeout match from two-level Ok(Ok/Err) to flat Ok(result)

Storage resilience (storage.rs):
- RunStore::open now retries on SQLite "busy" errors with linear backoff,
  handling transient lock contention from concurrent agent access
- atomic_rename and atomic_write_bytes now pre-delete the destination file
  before fs::rename for cross-device and Windows compatibility
- New test: atomic_rename_overwrites_existing_destination_file

Sync cursor upgrade (sync.rs):
- Upgrade incremental export cursor from timestamp-only to (finished_at, id)
  composite key, resolving duplicate/missing exports when multiple runs
  share identical timestamps
- SyncCursor gains last_export_run_id: Option<String> (backward-compatible)
- export_table_runs_incremental, collect_exported_run_ids, and
  max_export_position (renamed from max_finished_at) all use composite
  ordering
- atomic_rename and atomic_write_bytes get the pre-delete fix from storage
- New tests: legacy cursor deserialization, same-timestamp export ordering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
